mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions into higher S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is valid when the policy rate is declining or already below a recent peak; it fails when rate cuts coincide with recessionary labor deterioration that overwhelms valuation support.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods with a 1-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's 3-year rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above recent trend forces tighter real-rate expectations and multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when inflation surprises while policy rates are still near the effective lower bound or not yet adjusted upward.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-crossing episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if adding FEDFUNDS does not improve hit rate versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and lower future demand, which compresses earnings expectations and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is activated by a clear deterioration in unemployment rather than a stable high level.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative in triggered unemployment-deterioration windows, or if the triggered sample does not outperform unconditional baseline classification.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad domestic demand strength and housing-cycle momentum, which supports near-term revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when retail gains are offset by housing contraction or when both series flatten after a prior surge.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-growth condition does not yield positive 20-day SPX returns more often than 50%, or if one proxy alone explains the signal as well as the pair.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO indicates improving industrial supply-side activity and stronger cyclical earnings momentum, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect should be strongest when industrial production is rising while unemployment is stable or falling.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive after three-month INDPRO increases, or if the signal disappears when UNRATE is added as a control.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS reflects labor-market contraction that reduces aggregate income and future consumption, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The short-horizon effect is strongest when payroll losses coincide with stable or rising unemployment, confirming broadening labor weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll decline episodes are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the effect vanishes after conditioning on UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: A strong GDPC1 expansion indicates rising real output and improving broad demand, supporting corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is most informative when GDP growth is accelerating versus the prior quarter; it fails in post-recession rebounds where the market has already priced the improvement.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDP trend periods do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of observations, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate combined with elevated CPIAUCSL identifies a restrictive real-rate regime that suppresses valuation multiples and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The relationship fails when high rates are fully offset by rapidly falling inflation or when policy is already turning lower.
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-rate episodes do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the joint FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL rule offers no improvement over FEDFUNDS alone.
